message:
fix(mux-server): graceful SIGTERM handling with pending-state flush (ft-gqbpk)

Closes ft-gqbpk. The frankenterm-mux-server daemon's `run()` ended
with `loop { executor.tick()?; }` and no signal handler installed,
so SIGTERM triggered the default "terminate immediately" kernel
action. `run()` never returned → `main()` never reached its
`wezterm_blob_leases::clear_storage()` cleanup → pidfile lock
`std::fs::File` drop skipped → blob leases left in storage.

## Fix (narrow-scope, 30-min-cap appropriate)

Added the minimum async-signal-safe flag-poll plumbing at the top
of `main.rs`:

1. `static SHUTDOWN_REQUESTED: AtomicBool` — the one-bit signal
   from the handler to the poll loop.
2. `shutdown_requested()` / `request_shutdown()` /
   `reset_shutdown_flag_for_tests()` — thin accessors so the test
   suite can exercise the flag without raising real signals.
3. `shutdown_signal_handler(sig: c_int)` — async-signal-safe
   SIGTERM/SIGINT handler. Only operation: one relaxed-store
   atomic write. No allocation, no libc calls that aren't
   signal-safe, no Mux/lua/stdout work in signal context.
4. `install_shutdown_signal_handlers()` — registers the handler
   for SIGTERM + SIGINT via `libc::signal` on Unix, no-op on
   Windows (the daemonize path is already `#![cfg(unix)]`).

Wired into `run()`:
- Call `install_shutdown_signal_handlers()` BEFORE entering the
  executor loop so a signal during startup still routes through
  the poll path.
- Replace `loop { executor.tick()?; }` with
  `while !shutdown_requested() { executor.tick()?; }`.
- On loop exit: log the shutdown intent, call `Mux::shutdown()`
  to flush pending mux activity + let PTY/domain Drop
  implementations run, then return `Ok(())`. main() then
  proceeds through its existing success-branch clear_storage
  call.

No new dependency (uses existing `libc`; did not add
`signal-hook`). No change to `frankenterm-mux-server-impl`. The
fix is localized to a single binary crate.

## Tests (7 new)

All gated on `test_lock()` + a fresh-shutdown-state helper so the
global atomic can't bleed between cases:

- `shutdown_flag_starts_false` — fresh-state invariant.
- `request_shutdown_sets_flag` — direct accessor round-trip.
- `reset_shutdown_flag_for_tests_clears_flag` — helper hygiene.
- `shutdown_signal_handler_sets_flag_on_sigterm` — SIGTERM path
  through the handler function (called directly; no actual signal
  raised, so the test binary's own signal routing is safe).
- `shutdown_signal_handler_sets_flag_on_sigint` — SIGINT path.
- `shutdown_poll_loop_exits_after_request` — end-to-end mirror
  of the production `while !shutdown_requested()` loop. Asserts
  exit happens at the tick where `request_shutdown` fires, not
  one tick later (regression guard against accidentally using
  a stale flag read inside the loop body).
- `install_shutdown_signal_handlers_is_idempotent` — repeated
  install must not corrupt state; defensive guard in case
  re-exec or re-init ever calls it twice.

## Not in this fix (documented as follow-ups)

- Integration test that spawns the real `frankenterm-mux-server`
  binary, kills it with SIGTERM, and asserts exit code 0. That
  requires a config + sockets + TLS cert test harness that does
  not exist today; noted but deferred.
- Replacement of raw `libc::signal` with `sigaction` for
  portability across thread-safety models. `signal(2)` is
  sufficient for a single-threaded startup install with a
  flag-set handler body; if a future refactor starts installing
  handlers after workers spawn, this will need to migrate.
- `tokio::signal` / `signal-hook::iterator::Signals` idiomatic
  variants. Out of scope for a single-binary low-severity fix;
  `libc::signal` here is a deliberate "fewest moving parts"
  choice.

## Related

- ft-lokam (e790f481) — diag cwd privacy scrubber, same audit
  pass.
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — restored-command CR/LF sanitizer, same
  defense-in-depth theme ("clean exit / clean input at the
  boundary").
